input.prompt:
My ReportGenerator class uses DateTime.UtcNow and I can't write deterministic
unit tests for it. I'm on .NET 10. Can you create the right abstraction so
I can mock time in tests?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to make their `ReportGenerator` class testable by abstracting away `DateTime.UtcNow`. They're on .NET 10, which means they have access to `TimeProvider` — the built-in abstraction introduced in .NET 8. Let me first look at what's in the fixtures directory to understand the codebase.

data.content: Since you're on .NET 10, you already have `System.TimeProvider` built in — no need to create a custom abstraction. I'll inject it into `ReportGenerator`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is on .NET 10, which has `System.TimeProvider` built-in (introduced in .NET 8). This is the idiomatic way to abstract time in modern .NET. I should:

1. Inject `TimeProvider` into `ReportGenerator` via constructor injection
2. Replace `DateTime.UtcNow` with `timeProvider.GetUtcNow()`

This way, in production code they can use `TimeProvider.System` (the default), and in tests they can use `Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider` or just mock it.

Let me make the changes.

toolCalls.name: edit
